rationale: Both correctly diagnose the config/tokenizer mismatch, cite the same crash site, and recommend the DiT workaround plus a fail-fast guard/new tracking issue, matching the thread's resolution closely. X misattributes PR #2713 as having introduced the ratio-slice crash logic itself, when the thread actually cites #2713 only for changing the yaml's mode-filtering behavior — a grounding slip — and adds an unverified config table (i2t/t2t) not established in the thread. Y stays tighter to the thread's own claims, adds a concrete (if illustrative) guard patch and a plausible doc-mislabeling observation, and frames the close/follow-up split more precisely against Gaohan123's ask.
